title: Please add new pricing tiers between FREE to 25$
body: 25$ is not a piece of cake for everyone, especially for startups where FREE Quota is not enough and 25$ are more then enough. Please add a new pricing tier for 10$. This will remove the constraints of 2 org x 2 projects, and we will be able to host our hobby projects along with professional apps in a very affordable price.

title: Allow custom domains for API & Storage
body:
# Feature request

Hi!
I'd love to be able to set a custom domain alias for my supabase project url, so that instead:

![Screenshot from 2022-02-21 22-41-46](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/156725/155029544-c233c0e5-3204-42ce-8e39-1516ce0adaae.png)

the users would see "Chose an account to continue to myproject.mydomain.org".

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The google sign in will show: "Choose an account to continue to dalihgspogdhpodshhofdgogdssg.supabase.co which looks *completely like phishing* to anyone who received an 101 phishing-defense training.
**So this is not purely aesthetic problem, but a business one - imagine you prototype a product using Supabase (which it is great at) but you loose conversion because users are afraid to sign in** (being worried that some strange jfdsljfdsfuds.supabase.co people will get access to their precious Google account)! :fearful: 


## Describe the solution you'd like

Implement a basic custom domain support using LetsEncrypt free/automatic cert generation to secure it. Not hard to implement!

This would require:
 - [ ] setting custom domains in project settings
 - [ ] supabase proxy generating LetsEncrypt SSL cert to be able to serve the domain securely (alternatively, let user upload a commercial SSL cert)

## Describe alternatives you've considered

I did search GH issues to learn if you have considered custom domains and decided against them for *reasons*; but I could not find any such discussions.

## Additional context

None, thank you for great work and FLOSS generosity of your enterprise!

title: Local Studio - Feature Request for Saving Snippets Locally
body:
# Bug report

## Describe the bug
In the local studio dashboard, I cannot edit a SQL Query name

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. In the local studio, go to the SQL editor
2. Click "New query"
3. Do not edit the name, click elsewhere
4. Come back, you can no longer edit the name and it says "Untitled query"

## Expected behavior
I should be able to edit the query name

## Screenshots

![image](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/assets/9679076/ce368bd4-0ce0-4b41-91f7-9226eb27553e)

## System information

- OS: mac
- Browser (if applies): Brave
- Version of supabase-js:  @supabase/supabase-js": "^2.36.0
- Version of Node.js: v20.5.1

## Additional context

Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Also Add Password Changing Guidelines
body:
# Improve documentation

## Link 

https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting/docker

## Describe the problem

When password is changed like for postgres in .env , It causes an error when started again `docker compose up -d` , there is no mention of fixing that or any passwd changing guidelines.

## Describe the improvement

After many hours i found out  you need to `docker compose down -v` and also `rm -rf volumes/db/data/` sometimes. Then `docker compose up -d` to change it without error.

title: Add Push Notification Function that similar function to Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I see a limitations of supabase in terms of push notification, as i read in [Supabase Functions]( https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions/3588), that we need to configure more complicated than using Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)

## Describe the solution you'd like

I hope that supabase can add a Push Notification Function similar to Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM), so that we can notify client apps especially on mobile devices (android or ios) that we have a new update or a new message in our app.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

- Firebase Cloud Messaging

## Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

title: Allow Cascades while deleting or updating rows
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Right now, if we try to delete a row which is referenced in another table by foreign key, it results in an error. An optional cascade option should be available in case referencing rows are also to be deleted.

## Describe the solution you'd like
An option, like a toggle should be given in the Supabase Dashboard when creating and editing tables, that allows to enable/disable cascades for foreign keys.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

Deleting for referenced columns manually, but this required more calls to the api/database.

title: Add Feature to Test + Debug Row Level Security Policies
body:
Currently when writing a RLS policy you have to manually test it. They're also very difficult to debug when things go wrong as there is no logging (the current postgres logs are very cryptic and not suitable for debugging RLS policies.)

Some suggestions I have around this (not sure if they're doable though?):

1. Add the ability for unit tests or test cases for RLS policies
2. Add (better?) logging for debugging RLS policies. e.g. when an API call is made, it would be great to view with what values the RLS policy was executed and what was the output


----
The context behind this request is that Im currently writing a RLS policy that is not behaving as expected and I have no idea how to debug it effectively. I'm just shooting in the dark and modifying my code and rerunning my api calls in the hopes that it works

title: Custom domains for Storage Buckets
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I think it's important for nearly every website/webapp to have "brandable" and permanent public urls not only to pages, but also to picture assets (and other static assets). Currently Supabase does not offer a possibility to customize Storage Bucket public urls.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Storage Buckets should allow custom domain addresses, so for example, I could have a image at: `https://mydomain.com/logos/primary-logo.png` instead of currently `https://aosibqrvsopgfgnolphf.supabase.in/storage/v1/object/public/logos/primary-logo.png`

## Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently I am not using Storage Buckets for this reason and I had to configure my own S3 bucket with CloudFront and custom subdomain. The image paths on my root domain acts as proxy and masks the destination path (making it appear the user hasn't changed their location on the site).

title: Support signed hooks that can be verified to come from a trusted Supabase project
body:
# Feature request

Currently, the hooks sent to an endpoint are unsigned.

As we note in the [RedwoodJS webhook documentation](https://redwoodjs.com/docs/webhooks):

> If you were to subscribe to one of these webhooks, you'd point it to an endpoint in your RedwoodJS api -- ie, a serverless function. But, because that function is out "in the cloud" you need to ensure that these run only when they should. That means your function must:
>
>* verify it comes from the place you expect
>* trust the party
>* know the payload sent in the hook hasn't been tampered with
>* ensure that the hook isn't reprocessed or replayed (sometimes)
>
>That is, you need to verify your incoming webhooks.

To ensure that incoming webhooks are trustworthy, we verify webhooks and offer several, common and popular [methods to verify](https://redwoodjs.com/docs/webhooks#webhook-verification) them using signature verification:

> Common signature verification methods are:
> 
> SHA256 (GitHub and Discourse)
> SHA1 (Vercel)
> JWT (Netlify)
> Timestamp Scheme (Stripe / Redwood default)
> Secret Key (Custom, Orbit)

## Describe the solution you'd like

We'd want to:

* declare the header to use (perhaps `x-supabase-signature` by default?) and which can be customized. 
* perhaps include over headers to include the app name or details (to distinguish webhooks from multiple Supabase apps/projects)
* Pick a singing method (SHA256, SHA1, JWT) etc (Or perhaps default to the most common, SHA256)
* Sign the payload
* Send the hook to the endpoint with the headers

### Question - signing and secret key:

* What is the signing secret key?
* If you use the JWT_SECRET in Supabase, does that a) expose that in can be queried
* But, since typically the client is setup with the `JWT_SECRET` that makes it very convenient to use as a verification secret value
* We'd *never* want to pass it along via a JWT verifier as that would expose it to the public potentially

### Question - when to sign:

* Would we want to sign the payload when queuing the hook (ie set headers and put that signature in the headers when adding to the `net.http_request_queue`
* Sign when actually sending the hook off the queue

In the case of a timestamp signed webhook, having it set before sent from q could mean it is out of date if perhaps you are scheduling for the future or some delay happens -- bu that could also be a good thing (See how Strrip uses timestamped hooks in their [best practices](https://stripe.com/docs/webhooks/best-practices) ) because:

>The TimestampScheme verifier not only signs the payload with a secret (SHA256), but also includes a timestamp to prevent replay attacks and a scheme (i.e., a version) to further protect webhooks.

> A replay attack is when an attacker intercepts a valid payload and its signature, then re-transmits them. To mitigate such attacks, third-parties like Stripe includes a timestamp in the Stripe-Signature header. Because this times

title: Enable RLS by default for new tables
body:
For tables created from the dashboard, RLS is disabled by default. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2155545/149487648-fb68b680-5970-4e22-93eb-aef391390bb9.png)

Enabling it by default will improve security. We need to balance this with users forgetting to add a RLS policy and not getting any data back from the API. A good compromise would be to have an easy way to create a select policy for anon users when creating the table.

title: Ability to create local users easily
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When developing locally it can be a bit of a hassle to get a user to work. Even if you have a whole sign up flow, you still have to fiddle around with the Inbucket and it's not very clear how it works. Also when developing locally I like to have a script that initializes my whole environment (kinda like the `seed.sql` and `migrations.sql` are doing for the database), it would be very convenient if you could also easily populate the database with some test users.

## Describe the solution you'd like

1. Better documentation on the currrent situation. I had to do quite some digging to find out how to create a user locally without a signup flow and how I could get my email from Inbucket. It might be nice to have some docs on that (especially the email part with the _@inbucket.org_ adresses).
2. Have some convenient way to create test users locally (maybe with the Supabase CLI).

## Describe alternatives you've considered

I called the `auth.api.createUser` endpoint using the server only key to create a user, then went to Inbucket and confirmed the user (which only worked with an _@inbucket.org_ email). My app prototype does not a have a full sign up flow yet which I guess is going to be the case for more apps.

## Additional context

These are just suggestions and things I noticed. I'm curious on other views on this. I think at least "something" should be done to improve the local development with users.

title: User authentication on Nextjs SSR
body:
Currently, in the nextjs supabase example it is mentioned that the example shows client side and API route authentication. 

It would be helpful to also support user authentication for server side rendering in the "getServerSideProps" function. This would allow making authenticated requests to API routes during SSR.

https://github.com/supabase/supabase/tree/master/examples/nextjs-with-supabase-auth
> This example shows how to use Supabase auth both on the client (useUser hook) and server in API routes.

title: Local Supabase Studio development shows 500 errors when trying to view logs
body: # Bug report - [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. - [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). ## Describe the bug Supabase Studio for local development flow is not reliable at all. 1) I can't see logs. If I try to see Postgres logs (or any other logs) I get a 500 error. 2) When you go to Triggers and refresh the page I see a "Failed to retrieve database triggers" error. 3) If I create a trigger through SQL query I can't see it listed on the dashboard (although it is created and it does work). If I create a trigger through the dashboard I can see it on the dashboard. It's really hard to develop on a development/staging/production environment when the local development environment is so unstable. I'll need to either develop directly on production or look for a different solution. ## To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository: 1. Run supabase with `supabase start` 2. Open the dashboard. 3. Create a trigger through a sql query 4. Go to Triggers on the dashboard and look for the trigger, it won't be there. 5. Go to the Logs page. There will be a 500 error. ## Expected behavior I should be able to see all triggers, no matter if I create it through a query or through the dashboard/ui. I should be able to see the postgres logs on the dashboard. ## Screenshots ## System information - OS: MacOS Sonoma 14.0 (Intel Chipset) - Edge and Chrome - Version of supabase-js: 2.38.4 - Version of Node.js: 20.6.1 ## Additional context Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Impersonation (Auth Hook JWT) does not reflect production behaviour
body:
# Bug report

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

When using the Impersonation feature of Supabase Studio, it doesn't reflect the actual permissions as it does not call the defined **Custom Access Token Hook**.

This in fact is not a feature request but a bug because Supabase Studio by all means must reflect production behaviour when it comes to impersonation with regards to the authenticated user.

The original discussion is found here: https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/22482#discussioncomment-9011500 


## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Create a Custom Access Token Hook as follows:

```
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.custom_access_token_hook(event jsonb)
RETURNS jsonb
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
  DECLARE
    claims jsonb;
    user_id uuid;
  BEGIN
    user_id := (event->>'user_id')::uuid;

    claims := event->'claims';

    -- Check if 'app_metadata' exists in claims
    if jsonb_typeof(claims->'app_metadata') is null then
        -- If 'app_metadata' does not exist, create an empty object
        claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{app_metadata}', '{}');
    end if;


    -- Set a claim of 'admin'
    claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{app_metadata, combo}', '["snoo"]');

    -- Update the 'claims' object in the original event
    event := jsonb_set(event, '{claims}', claims);

    -- Return the modified or original event
    return event;
  END;
$$;

grant execute
  on function public.custom_access_token_hook
  to supabase_auth_admin;
```

2. Go to the SQL editor and impersonate _any_ authenticated user
3. Execute `SELECT auth.jwt();`
4. See `combo` property missing

## Expected behavior

With the given custom hook `combo: ["snoo"]` should show up in the user object of `SELECT auth.jwt()`.

title: Support API key generation
body:
# Feature request

Please support generating user-tied API keys via `supabase-js`/`GoTrue`

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I'm developing an open API. Users can sign into a web portal and generate API tokens to be used when communicating with the API. Users can generate multiple tokens, e.g. if they want to use different keys for Android and iOS client applications and track their usage separately.

Currently, I can find no good way of generating such tokens/API keys without exposing secrets or breaking RLS.

## Describe the solution you'd like

It should be possible to create an "authorized token"/"api key"/other static string for signed in users. Such tokens could be stored in an `access_tokens(user_id uuid, access_token varchar, created_at timezonetz)` table in the `auth` schema. This token should then be possible to supply in the request header instead of a jwt when accessing supabase. Queries using this token should be subject to the same RLS policies as if the user's jwt token was sent. Additionally, it should be possible to discern if the request was made using an API key and in that case, which one. This could be done e.g. by introducing `auth.api_key()` and would allow me to create additional tables and RLS policies to limit the access a given API key provides (e.g. "this token can only be used to read the user's public git repositories") myself.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

1. Using my service key to violate RLS on a custom, protected, `api_keys(user uuid, key varchar)` table (still cannot impersonate a user + RLS is now useless),
2. minting my own JWTs (hard, error prone and feels like asking for trouble), or
3. Limiting access to generic access only (using anon key, and forcing me to keep this key more secret).

## Additional context

#4419

title: auth.signIn() with magic link creates users in Auth.users table
body:
# Bug report

## Describe the bug

Using magic link feature to login creates the user if the email provided doesn't exist on Auth.users table.

## To Reproduce

1. Use this snippet to login with magic lik

`const { user, session, error } = await supabase.auth.signIn({
  email: 'example@email.com'
})`

2. Use an email that doesn't exist on Auth.users table
3. Check Auth.users table

## Expected behavior

If the email provided is not in Auth.users table it shouldn't be created and an error should be thrown.

title: Standardize Error Responses across APIs
body:
As suggested by Nicolo, we should standardize the error responses coming from GoTrue, postgres, and realtime (and any other/future APIs) so that it's better DX when writing a client,

We can make this change on the servers themselves, but since postgrest and gotrue are fully/partially external may be harder to change, it might be an option to transform the errors within the client libraries/supabase-js, could be messy?

Nicolo also dropped this as a reference: http://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#openapi-specification

title: Expose PostgREST's `pre-request` config option to enable revoking long-lived tokens
body:
# Feature request

Expose PostgREST's `pre-request` config option to enable revoking long-lived tokens

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

This issue is based on this discussion: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions/3205

There is more detail in the discussion linked above, but to summarize my use case:
1. I'd like to give long-lived JWTs to users of my webapp to use as API keys (akin to Stripe tokens or Supabase's `service_role` token)
2. I'd like to let users revoke and regenerate these tokens in my webapp's UI (so they can handle security issues on their end, like leaks or employee offboarding)
3. Supabase currently has no way to apply some security middleware (e.g. to check "is this request's token/claim in my list of revoked tokens?")

There are similar use cases, like this discussion https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions/2265 about wanting to revoke a `service_role` JWT (without needing to contact supabase support to do so).

## Describe the solution you'd like

As suggested in [PostgREST's docs on revocation](https://postgrest.org/en/v8.0/tutorials/tut1.html#bonus-topic-immediate-revocation), it would be nice to have a `pre-request` function that can run for all API requests. Maybe this option could be exposed in the Supabase Admin UI as a new API setting? Or, maybe this option could be specified in the database via [PostgREST's In-Database Configuration](https://postgrest.org/en/v8.0/configuration.html#in-database-configuration), though this method seems to require SUPERUSER.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

As described in the github discussion (https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions/3205), an alternative would be to add a revocation-check policy to every table, which is not-DRY and is error-prone due to the duplication, as well as bad for performance (if a single request makes many queries, or even a single query across many tables, the revocation check is evaluated repeatedly).

Other alternatives I can think of would be even worse (e.g. putting a revocation-checking proxy service in front of the Supabase API, sending all API requests to some database function that does revocation-checking then dispatches to other tables, e.g. `/rpc/dispatcher?table=profiles&payload=...`).

## Additional context

It looks like y'all are already working on exposing other PostgREST config options, like `db-schema` according to this discussion: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions/1222

Implementing this feature request would hopefully share a lot of the same work and be not-too-onerous as a result! :crossed_fingers:

title: Poor BigInt support
body:
This is a bit of a meta-bug, as it were. This issue seems to be swept under the rug as no big deal, but the crux of the issue here is BigInt, or int8, is a base postgres type that breaks in numerous ways when trying to use it in supabase. 64 bit numbers have beein in common use for ~20 years.

BigInt is used in numerous id schemes as it indexes well, can be used as a sort mechanism, and converted to convenient strings for URLs. It is widely used in Supabase own docs as an id column. But if you try to use it, your app can break in confusing ways (no error is returned, and id is incorrect) or you have to use annoying workarounds (convert your id to a string then to a BigInt on every select? Really?)
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/74549311/how-to-use-bigint-safely-with-supabase-js

The BigInt type is supported by every browser as well as Typescript.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/BigInt
https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/release-notes/typescript-3-2.html#bigint

And yet when you generate types with supabase it generates them as number.
https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/issues/863#issuecomment-1835478711

And if you select one larger than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER it will get rounded. This affects the dashboard as well. Despite bugs getting fixed to allow you to input a BigInt in the UI, and sort by them, it will still DISPLAY them incorrectly. No errors, just inaccurate data.

Despite it, again, being a base type in postgres, it is frustrating to many users as is evidenced by the multitude of bugs in various states related.

https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/issues/863
https://github.com/supabase/postgrest-js/issues/319
https://github.com/supabase/postgrest-js/issues/273
https://github.com/supabase/postgrest-js/issues/419

And a host of others closed as duplicates. I recognize this will get immediately closed, but I hope it draws attention to a gaping hole in supabase tools.

title: Add a section to javascript API documentation on conditionally building queries.
body:
# Improve documentation

## Link 

https://supabase.com/docs/reference/javascript/using-filters

supabase/web/spec/supabase.yml in repository.

## Describe the problem

Several of us have been asked/answered in Discord on how to deal with conditional filters.  For instance having option to search several different columns, but only use the ones filled in by the user.  It is not obvious you can build up the query and then execute it with await or .then() from any Supabase examples I'm familiar with. 

## Describe the improvement

I suggest adding this to the end of the using filter 'page'.  I've attempted to write it in the format of the yaml file, but I'm not familiar with it.  OF COURSE THE CODE BLOCK HERE ATE the "3 backticks" JS at start and "3 backticks" at end of the js example....

`
###Conditionally Chaining Filters

Filters can be built up one step at a time and then executed.

```js
const filterByName = null
const filterPopLow = 1000
const filterPopHigh = 10000

let query = supabase
	.from('cities')
	.select('name, country_id')
	
if (filterByName) {query = query.eq('name',filterByName)}
if (filterPopLow) {query = query.gte('population', filterPopLow)}
if (filterPopHigh) {query = query.lt('population', filterPopHigh)}

const { data, error } = await query
```
`


## Additional context

I'm very new to Github repositories and don't feel comfortable experimenting on a pull request here myself.

title: Add light mode to the admin page
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I'm using light mode in a lot of my web browsing. Switching to supabase's forced dark mode can be inconvenient for the eyes for us light mode users.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Add an option in the settings for a light mode.

title: Universal Login - Share login session between different subdomains.
body:
# Feature request

Universal Login. I'd like to share the login session between different subdomains.

## Describe the solution you'd like

I don't know much about that, but I've heard there are two general ways.

1. Using cookies like [SuperTokens](https://supertokens.io).
2. Local Storage + iframe like [Auth0](https://auth0.com).

If there are other alternatives, I'd like to know about them.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

The problem is the relationship between Authentication and Project is one-to-one. This is also the case in firebase. If we want to implement Universal Login, we have to take another option.

Supabase is really useful and has great potential. With Universal Login, Supabase will be able to handle more use cases.

title: Improve row level subscription documentation
body:
# Improve documentation

## Link 

https://supabase.com/docs/reference/javascript/subscribe#listening-to-row-level-changes

## Describe the problem

The documentation specifies the ability to listen on "individual rows", giving the example of listening to a "country" by it's "id".

I assume "id" is a primary/unique key in this case.

The documentation isn't clear on whether this "eq" syntax is an exact row match on a primary/unique key, or more of a "filter" that will match any row that equals a certain value.

For example, I want to know if I can use this to listen on "todos" with a foreign key column of "user_id" to be notified of all "todos" of a user that update, not a single/ "individual row".

 Is this possible? It's not clear from the documentation, and is why I'm writing this issue.

## Describe the improvement

Specify that the eq only works for primary keys or specify whether it's a filter (albeit limited) that can return events that "match" the "filter".

title: Feature: Environments
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Currently, there is no way to switch between dev, staging, & prod (for my use case, I'm sure different envs for different people).

## Describe the solution you'd like

I'd love to hit different URIs for different envs. I doubt I need to go into more detail but when I ship a feature, I want it to go to staging before prod. QA can do their thing but not affect prod data, etc. When it's time, I'd like to transition staging to prod. 

## Describe alternatives you've considered

I haven't tried it but I suppose I could manually create different projects, manually replicating the database, etc. This isn't scalable or efficient though

## Additional context

I feel like this is pretty self-explanatory but if I need to add more details, I can. I think the simplest example would be Heroku but really, most any major platforms have some variant of this

title: Allow hooks (and triggers) be declared on schemas other than public (such as auth and storage)
body:
# Feature request

Allow hooks (and triggers) be declared on schemas other than public (such as auth and storage)

For example, when a file is uploaded to storage or a user is updated?

Reason I ask is that within a RedwoodJS app, uploading and updating a user would likely be done using the Supabase client  (or maybe Postgrest) and not use a direct (pooled) connection to Postgres using Prisma.

Therefore, a webhook response back to the Redwood API (to a Serverless function that accepts an incoming Webhook) is super useful.

On the api side, one could then retrieve the updated records and handle that accordingly.

Common scenarios:

* User signup
* User profile (metadata) update
* File uploaded completed
* File updated in storage

And a simple feature would be for Redwood to send a crafted message to Slack (or something) with that webhook record content.

Currently only public schemas are available:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1051633/128027244-10e3d49a-e614-412d-bbdd-0b08c2268899.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1051633/128027277-ec06f653-303e-47b4-ad23-bba544f53308.png)

title: Offline Support
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

An app for employees accessing a database "in the wild" where internet access is sparse (Germany is a third world country in that regard).

## Describe the solution you'd like

Ideally there would be the same functionality as with Firestore. Modify database locally, syncs back to server once a connection is available. Querying cached data should work out of the box with no client side code necessary. Should be enabled by default.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently considering CouchDB, Parse, Kuzzle, SapphireDB and Appwrite. Using Firebase directly isn't possible for this project due to Open Source requirements and GDPR requirements.

## Additional context

Firebase offers Firestore with really good offline-first support and data sync. Since this project is supposed to be a Firebase alternative, will this ever be supported? I couldn't find anything in the documentation regarding this.

title: Being able to set the user session duration
body:
# Feature request
By default user sessions currently last 1 hour (3600s). 

To save time and effort to the user, it would make sense for us to be able to configure the session duration.

title: [Website, UX] Confusingly named "login" button
body: # Bug report ## Describe the bug On the Supabase website, what is effectively the "login" button is named "Start your project" which can be confusing / dissonant when unconsciously [Thin Slicing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thin-slicing) upon page load and expecting to see some derivation of the common `[Login] [Sign up!]` top-right button pattern. _(In this case "Sign up!" being "Start your project")_ ### Current screenshot Naturally, from a technical perspective given Supabase uses GitHub OAuth, both buttons end up at the same place, but from a UX perspective having a named "Login" button would reduce the dissonance. The "Star us on Github" button is currently occupying this space and several times I've accidently clicked it when navigating quickly as I've unconsciously pattern matched it as the "Login" button. ## Suggestion To retain the prominence of the "Star us on Github" button, it could be subordinated to the left and restyled with ```html ``` ## System information - OS: Windows + OSX - Browser (if applies) Any browser using a desktop display resolution. ## Edit _edit: upon the last five minutes of reflection, this is by far the most autistic issue / rant I've ever written so absolutely close if this is not a priority._ 🤦🏻‍♂️😂

title: signup without password?
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

It looks like there's the ability to use magic links to log in. But I can't figure out a way to have a user sign up without requiring a password in the beginning. If they provide and email, confirm it, and then always use magic links, why require a password at all?

### Describe the solution you'd like

Ideally the `password` option would be optional for sign up.

title: Custom email links
body: # Feature request When I invite a user to signup they receive an invitation link. This link is within the supabase.co domain. I would like to customize this link masking it with my own domain. This should increase the trust for visitors clicking on a link with a familiar domain name

title: use AccessToken with custom claims and definable 3rd party signing key
body:
# Feature request

Right now it seems the token needed for Auth against the supabase DB is quite custom. I looked at Hasura and there its so much easier to work with a 3rd party auth service like Auth0 because you can just use the AccessToken from Auth0 with some custom Claims in it. 

## Describe the solution you'd like

I want to put the signage key of the token (i get from auth0) in the Supabase admin section. And i want the needed things for supabase in namespaced custom claims inside the token. This can be easily done with Auth0 (actions). With that i can just pass the AccessToken (JWT compliant) of Auth0 to Supabase. 

Supabase can do the Token verification easily with the provided key. All in all i dont need a cloud function with generates me a Supabase compliant JWT, which is always a hassle because the mentioned cloud function service also needs to auth against my current Auth0 user. So a lot of headaches. 

## Describe alternatives you've considered

I checked Hasura for a good way to do it. 
See https://hasura.io/learn/graphql/nextjs-fullstack-serverless/auth0-setup/ for a no-brainer how to integrate Auth0 with Hasura without the need to create custom JWTs.

title: Support for bun.js runtime
body:
Hi! It would be awesome if supabase supported this new speedy runtime. 

I've attempted to see if it will run, but it does not. 

My file is basic:
``` javascript
import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"

const supabaseUrl = process.env.SUPABASE_URL
const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

export const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```

After, I ran and received this:
``` bash
➜  myproject git:(main) ✗ bun run test.ts
[0.06ms] ".env"
1 | // constants.ts
2 | import { version } from './version';
   ^
 ReferenceError: Can't find variable: require
at ..../node_modules/@supabase/supabase-js/dist/module/lib/constants.js:2:0
```

cheers

title: Edge functions should support outgoing connections to SMTP ports 25, 465, and 587.
body:
# Feature request

From the Edge Function guide, and another user's first attempt with the edge functions, it appears ports 25,465,587 do not work in Supabase deploy of Deno functions.  

If this is true then the ability to handle email tasks from an edge function are not possible.

## Describe the solution you'd like

If this is a correct interpretation of the limitation, is this a short term limitation?

Support of ability to send emails seems a common requirement for "serverless" functions.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

I currently use Firebase cloud functions to generate a pdf from database data and send via email as attachment at request of client code.  I also have a function to send email invites to chat rooms, also at the request of the user.  I want to move these functions over to my Supabase environment.  

## Additional context

title: When configuring custom authentication email templates, ensure template content is valid when saving
body: # Feature request When setting up a custom authentication email template as seen here: ![Screen Shot 2021-11-18 at 7 08 23 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1051633/142415178-adc96e41-d058-4828-92e3-e2743e9f7066.png) there isn't any information about what variable token replacements are allowed in each email. While there is some dev documentation in [GoTrue available](https://supabase.io/docs/gotrue/server/about) it sin't easy to reference. For example: ``` `MAILER_TEMPLATES_CONFIRMATION` - `string` URL path to an email template to use when confirming a signup. `SiteURL`, `Email`, and `ConfirmationURL` variables are available. ``` Only those three variables are allowed. However, the user may still save the template even if it is invalid due to improper formatting or using invalid variables. ![Screen Shot 2021-11-18 at 7 24 37 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1051633/142416368-5a2b3a90-a8d1-4b21-b0dc-80491df27973.png) There was a case where a user added a replacements ` and what happens is the template is saved, but when GoTrue fetches the template to send outgoing email, if fails to parse and the default template content is used. See: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/web/docs/gotrue/server/about.md ## Describe the solution you'd like * Perhaps ad help or some info for what variables are permitted per template * Template validation on save to ensure template is ok to be used when actually sending mail. * Validation incudes syntax for the variable replacement (ie enclosed in double braces) and properly recognized variable names * Show a preview with mocked data * Send a preview email with mocked data ## Describe alternatives you've considered * Validate in Studio app * Validate via a new GoTrue endpoint This possible test case in [Netlify's `mailme` which GoTrue](https://github.com/netlify/mailme) uses to fetch and cache the template raises the error if the template is invalid ``` package mailme import ( "html/template" "testing" "github.com/sirupsen/logrus" ) func TestGet(t *testing.T) { var logger = logrus.New() var cache = &TemplateCache{ templates: map[string]*MailTemplate{}, funcMap: map[string]interface{}{}, logger: logger, } var temp *template.Template data, err := cache.Get("https://app.supabase.io/api/auth//templates/magic-link") if temp != nil { logger.Info(data) } if err != nil { logger.Error(err) t.Fail() } } ``` ## Additional context See also https://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/3944 and https://github.com/supabase/gotrue/issues/278#issuecomment-971122395

title: Supabase Client Playground
body:
# Feature request
A playground to quickly test supabase client without writing/setting up frontend code first, and must be able to connect to actual database to quickly iterate on development.

title: Generate entity relationship diagrams
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When working with a large database with dozens of tables and foreign key relationships, entity relationship (ER) diagrams really help with understanding how the database operates.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Generate an ER diagram automatically, and keep it up-to-sync with actual database.

For instance, in the dashboard in the Database section, add a new tab called "Diagram." This would then be the visual representation of the database.

Does not need to be editable.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

You could always create one by hand, but automatic documentation like this is always better than manual documentation (assuming the quality is the same)

## Additional context

While it would be helpful, I do think functions and local development migrations are a higher priority, personally. Would not be disappointed if this feature is never added, but incredibly excited it if is.

title: Asymmetric JWT Keys in Self Hosted Supabase
body:
# Bug report

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

Asymmetric JWT Signing keys and `PUBLISHABLE` JWT keys have been released for a while on Supabase, but they do not seem to be available/documented in the self hosted version. According to [this blog post](https://supabase.com/blog/jwt-signing-keys) they were launched in 2025 Jul.

However, the [Docker Compose YML](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/docker/docker-compose.yml) and the [self hosting documentation](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting/docker#generate-and-configure-api-keys) still refer to anon/service role keys, and symmetric JWT secrets/signing keys. 

How does one go about using the new JWT Signing keys in a self-hosted deployment?

## To Reproduce



- Visit documentation at https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting/docker#generate-and-configure-api-keys
- Notice that it is still using symmetric JWT Signing keys, as well as anon and service_role keys which should be replaced by publishable and secret keys
- Check the Docker Compose YML configuration at https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/docker/docker-compose.yml
- The configuration is also for legacy symmetric signing keys & JWT keys


## Expected behavior

Feature parity with the self hosted version with respect to asymmetric JWT keys




## Additional context

N/A

title: Fine grained access roles for organization team members
body:
# Feature request

Currently all users in a organisation are admin users. We need more fine grained access roles for collaborators within an organisation.

To start off with, we can have something basic like

* admin access
* read only access
* billing access

For the community: please add additional access controls that would be useful for you below.

title: Move docker directory to it's own repository
body:
# Chore

## Describe the chore

Move `docker` directory (needed for docker setup) to it's own repository

## Additional context

This is a _very_ large repository and if someone wants to try out self-hosted docker set up, they need to clone it entirely. Cloning with `--depth 1`, I managed to let it download about ~400MBs before giving up and manually saving the files for docker set-up.

This is also a pain point for server deployments where users may not want to store the entire supabase source tree. Updating supabase also means pulling all the _source_ changes since the last update, which is also undesirable, especially in a case if an automation script is polling for updates.

title: Add Delete Rule to GUI when setting Relationships
body: # Feature request Right now, when using the GUI to add a relationship between tables you cannot set the delete rule (e.g. cascade). You almost always want to specify the delete rule on the relationship when creating it, but this limitation in the GUI means that effectively the GUI has no use when it comes to setting relationship and you always need to use the SQL editor instead. See attached screenshot of current GUI:

title: Improve docs around backups
body:
We don't have a backups section, would be good to create a dedicated section with reference to:
- how often Supabase takes backups on the platform
- how these can be restored
- how to take your own backups using pg_dump / pg_restore
- what is PITR and how to get it on the platform
- etc.

title: Cascade Delete from UI
body: Allow setting cascade delete on foreign keys

title: Allow login sessions longer than 1 week
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Having to log in again and again is annoying, and I'm really glad most websites don't log me out after just a week of absence. Supabase login sessions unfortunately seem to be limited to that.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Ability to specify longer login sessions

title: Feat: Allow redirecTo use dynamic URIs
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Yes, I have a dynamic URL, which is `https://mysite.com/users/123`. 

Adding `https://mysite.com/users` to `Additional Redirect URIs`, I am able to redirect to `https://mysite.com/users` using the `redirectTo` option when trying to use magic links, but as you can see, I need to redirect to a specific user (e.g. An user with ID equals to `123`). 

For now, I can't redirect to the user directly to the page profile using Magic Links on signIn.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Be able to add dynamic URIs on `Additional Redirect URIs`

## Describe alternatives you've considered

Created a special page for it to deal it on the client-side, but it also created multiple redirects to the user, breaking the "magic" of magic links UX to redirect the user directly to the page that the user needs to see as soon as the user reaches the website.

title: Can't delete rows from Supabase UI
body:
# Bug report

## Describe the bug

I am unable to delete rows via dashboard (using cloud version of Supabase not selfhosted). 

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Go to table editor > tables > your table
2. Click on 'delete rows'
3. refresh

## Expected behavior

The rows should be deleted

## Video


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/49107150/153769989-16ad1e35-5f1a-4aef-b25e-50f9e587b4dd.mp4



## System information

- OS: Windows 11
- Browser: Chrome
- Version of supabase-js: cloud dashboard

title: Basic login on studio
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Add a _very_ basic login screen to the OSS screen

## Describe the solution you'd like

Let's pull this out into a new JSON config file:

https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/studio/pages/api/profile/index.ts

something basic like this:

```js
users : [
 { username: 'demo' },
 { username: 'hieu' }
]
organizations: [
 id: 1,
 projects: []
]
```

The users will be able modify this themselves. The config file should not be accessible from the frontend

## Describe alternatives you've considered

We could have a more robust auth system (OAuth etc), but thats beyond the scope of our open source offering

## Additional context

Discussed here:
https://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/4934

title: Allow me to invite users to my team even if they aren't registered with Supabase
body: # Feature request ## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. If I want to add someone to my Supabase team, they have to already have an account. If they don't have a supabase account, I get this error message: This is annoying because now I have to go to my teammate, ask them to sign up to supabase, give me the email they signed up with, then I have to go back and add them. This is very slow. I just want to be able to invite and then forget about it. ## Describe the solution you'd like Three potential solutions: 1. You let me invite them even though they don't have a supabase acount 2. You give me a custom invite link that I can share with the teammate(s) that they can use at any time to auto join the team 3. You let me associate a domain name (in our case, fig.io) with our team. This would mean that anyone with a fig.io email gets automatically added to our team upon signing up to supabase.

title: Custom claims
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Adding custom claims on the JWT token like in firebase.
This would make multi tenancy and user roles easier to implement with RLS

## Describe the solution you'd like

The ability to add custom claims to the JWT token send to the user upon sign in.
For example one or multiple organisation ID's a user belongs to.

This would greatly simplify and speed up RLS policy creation, since the org_id from the token can be directly compared to the org_id of the row without doing an additional query.

eg. what currently uses an additional select query
```
create policy "Read messages if user is a member of the org" 
  on public.messages 
  for select using ( 
    auth.uid() in (
        select user_id from members where members.org_id = messages.org_id
    )
  );
```
  

 Would become a simple policy we could add in the web ui
```

auth.claims.organisation = org_id
```


A realtime listener or polling system could be implemented in the client library to automatically refresh the token in case a users custom claims would change.

title: Verify access token on node.js
body:
# Add functionality to verify the access token and get a user on the server-side. 

## Firebase Admin-like API to verify id token and get the current user on the server-side. 

I'm now working on a Nest.js-based Graphql API server and trying out Supabase Auth. When use sends a request to the server, I need to verify the access token and pass down the user info in the middleware. I checked the API but it seems there's no functionality for that right now. I also checked gotrue-js but seems they don't have anything like that. Do you plan to add firebase-admin-like toolsets? 

## Describe the solution you'd like

With FirebaseAdmin: 

admin.auth()verifyIdToken() will verify the user based on the access token and return the currentFirebaseUser. It seems gotrue server is doing this but I like to do the same on my own server. 

Is there any workaround for now to verify the user on the node.js side?

title: Automatic Created and Updated columns
body:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
No specific problem right now, but it is a very common and useful feature.

## Describe the solution you'd like
When creating a table allow to add "Created" and "Updated" columns, which will be populated by the system accordingly

## Describe alternatives you've considered
Do in client side code for each table for each INSERT / UPDATE
